reason_for_decision: The article passes because it makes elevation information operationally concrete and shows why the added dimension changes ambiguity, cueing, and track quality.
strengths: The article explains 2D and 3D radar through downstream effects on tracking and cueing instead of through display terminology alone.
It is especially strong on where plan-view overlap and altitude ambiguity change operational outcomes.
The section on when 2D is still enough keeps the comparison balanced and credible.
summary_assessment: This is a clear pass under the strict rubric. It offers strong technical credibility and good decision support for teams choosing between 2D and 3D architectures.
